summary: Added validation-only M3 character, relationship, office, landed-power, policy, and enfeoffment-hook fixture support across content schema, runtime, compiler, CLI validation, and focused tests. The fixture compiles deterministically to a runtime pack with 64 FICTIONAL validation characters, 18 sparse directed relationships, 4 offices, 4 landed powers, 4 persistent office policies, and 4 enfeoffment hooks.

tests_run.result: Showed modified allowed task/content/test files plus pre-existing allowed goal/task/thread state changes; content-source/m3-fixtures/ is new.
Validated M1, M2, and M3 fixtures. M3 fixture output: characters=64 relationships=18 offices=4 landedPowers=4 officePolicies=4 enfeoffmentHooks=4 manifestHash=76b75849.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-CHARACTER-OFFICE-FIXTURE-001 and new fixture manifests
pnpm --filter @monsoon/content-schema typecheck && pnpm --filter @monsoon/content-runtime typecheck && pnpm --filter @monsoon/content-tools typecheck
tests_run.result: PowerShell rejected && as a statement separator in this environment; reran the three typecheck commands separately and all passed.

decisions: Implemented M3 character-office as validation-only FICTIONAL data with abstract source IDs and displayNameKeys; no formal historical content-source data or real-world names/titles/claims were added.
Kept relationship storage sparse and directed; compiler/runtime reject missing relationship references, self relationships, and parent ancestry cycles.
Modeled office policies as office-referenced definitions with persistsAcrossHolderChange=true and independent enfeoffmentHookIds, so changing currentHolderCharacterId does not erase policy metadata.
Extended content:validate to include M1, M2, and the new M3 fixture in deterministic compiler validation.
risks: This is schema/runtime/compiler fixture support only; sim-core appointment mechanics, protocol commands, save schema integration, and UI are intentionally untouched and remain future tasks.
The content-runtime file grew substantially because it follows the existing single-file hand-written validator pattern; reviewer may request later refactoring under a separate task.
requested_action: Review R3 schema/runtime/compiler fixture support for architecture boundaries, deterministic stable IDs/hashes, historical-policy safety, and test adequacy.
